source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A block of aluminium of mass 670 g is heated at a constant rate of 95 W for 6.0 minutes. The specific heat capacity of aluminium is $910 \mathrm{Jkg}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$. The initial temperature of the block is $24^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. In practice, there are energy losses to the surroundings. The actual variation with time $t$ of the temperature $\theta$ of the block is shown in Fig. 1.1. Using Fig. 1.1, calculate the total energy loss to the surroundings during the heating process.
